acuity-middleware publishes as `@tummycrypt/scheduling-bridge`.

Purpose:
- remote Acuity browser automation
- Effect-based browser/page lifecycle management
- HTTP bridge endpoints for services, availability, slots, and booking
- migration bridge between Acuity and future homegrown scheduling

Boundary:
- owns Playwright automation and remote deployment
- does not own Vercel app deployment
- does not own application-specific admin UI
- does not own reusable scheduling UI components

Deployment truth:
- Modal is the primary remote deployment surface
- Docker should mirror the same built server entrypoint
- app deploys and bridge deploys are separate release surfaces
- promotion analysis must check both app revision and bridge revision

Current tracking:
- milestone: Sprint T: Remote Acuity Performance & Release Control
- issues:
  - #20 tail latency and remaining performance work
  - #21 release control / deploy coordination

Architecture rails:
- prefer warm browser reuse with request-scoped pages
- prefer URL-based direct reads when possible
- retry false-empty Acuity reads carefully
- keep shared service catalog logic centralized
- use Effect where resource lifecycle correctness matters

Important commands:
- pnpm typecheck
- pnpm test
- pnpm build

Files to inspect first:
- src/server/handler.ts
- src/shared/browser-service.ts
- src/shared/acuity-service-catalog.ts
- src/adapters/acuity/wizard.ts
- src/adapters/acuity/steps/**
- modal-app.py
- Dockerfile
- .github/workflows/ci.yml
- .github/workflows/publish.yml
